title: Medical Physicist — Boron Neutron Capture Therapy (BNCT)
about: ASPL Fusion (Agnira Sanlayan Pvt. Ltd.) is an applied plasma and neutron-systems company working on accelerator-based BNCT, domestic medical-isotope production, and industrial neutron applications, within established Indian regulatory and safety frameworks. The clinical-physics lead is the bridge between the accelerator/BSA engineering and the cancer-treatment workflow — commissioning the treatment room, leading dosimetry for the Phase I trial in recurrent head-and-neck cancer (rH&NC), and training clinical staff.
description: Be the clinical-physics lead for ASPL's BNCT programme. Commission the treatment room, develop treatment protocols, lead dosimetry for Phase I clinical trials, and train clinical staff. This role works alongside oncologists, patients, and engineers daily.
responsibilities: Commission the BNCT treatment room (neutron-beam characterisation, in-phantom dosimetry)
Develop BNCT treatment protocols for the Phase I clinical trial (rH&NC)
Perform patient-specific treatment planning using the IGRT TPS
Lead clinical dosimetry — boron-concentration measurement, neutron-fluence verification
Collaborate with CDSCO on clinical-trial regulatory documentation
Train clinical staff (oncologists, nurses, technicians) on BNCT procedures
Publish clinical results as part of the standard publication workflow
requirements: M.Sc or PhD in Medical Physics
Understanding of neutron dosimetry (preferred — we will train the right candidate)
preferred: Prior BNCT experience (rare globally — strongly welcomed)
benefits: Co-authorship on ASPL publications and patent filings (the default for technical roles)
Conference travel support (ANS, IEEE NPSS, IPAC, ASTRO, IAEA technical meetings and equivalent)
Direct mentorship from Prof. Prabhat Ranjan and Prof. Y.C. Saxena, plus weekly working-group technical syncs
Founding-team scope: shape your domain end-to-end as part of a small team scaling up
Core collaboration hours 10:00–18:00 IST with flexibility outside that window
